title: Harden agent-to-agent chat reliability (P1-P5)
description:
Fix five reliability gaps in the Moe chat system so bots/agents can communicate dependably with each other.

**P1** `chat_send` reports `routed: message.mentions` (raw @-strings) instead of the actual expanded routing targets computed by MentionRouter. Agents cannot confirm who received their message.

**P2** Group mention expansion (@architects, @workers, @qa) uses dead-code stub + ID-substring fallback. Should use team.role lookup via Worker.teamId.

**P3** `chat_send` accepts any string as workerId — unregistered agents can impersonate known workers. Should validate sender is a registered worker (or the literal "human"/"system").

**P4** `chat_who` channel filter only counts workers that have a chatCursors entry for that channel; workers using only chat_wait + chat_send are invisible. Should also include workers currently blocked in chat_wait for that channel.

**P5** No MCP tool exposes StateManager.createChannel() — agents cannot create ad-hoc coordination channels. Needs a new `moe.chat_create_channel` tool.
definitionOfDone: chat_send returns routingTargets (expanded worker IDs) not raw mentions
@architects/@workers/@qa group mentions resolve via team.role, not ID substring
chat_send rejects unknown senders with invalidInput error (human and system are always allowed)
chat_who with channel filter includes workers currently in chat_wait for that channel
moe.chat_create_channel tool exists, creates custom channel, registered in tools/index.ts
All changes have no TypeScript type errors (tsc --noEmit passes)

implementationPlan.description:
P1 — Return expanded routing targets from chat_send.

In `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatSend.ts`:
- After calling `state.sendMessage()`, the returned ChatMessage only has raw `mentions` (parsed @-strings). But `sendMessage()` internally calls `mentionRouter.route()` and emits `routingTargets` on the event — we need those targets returned to the caller too.
- Modify `StateManager.sendMessage()` in `packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts` to return `{ message: ChatMessage, routingTargets: string[] }` instead of just `ChatMessage`. The routing targets are already computed internally — just propagate them out.
- Update `chatSend.ts` handler to destructure the new return value and return `routingTargets` (the expanded worker IDs) instead of `routed: message.mentions`.
- The response shape becomes: `{ success, messageId, channel, timestamp, routingTargets: string[] }` (rename `routed` → `routingTargets` for clarity).
- Add error handling: if mentionRouter throws, fall back to raw mentions as routingTargets.
- Test: write a vitest test that sends a message with `@all` and verifies `routingTargets` contains expanded worker IDs, not the literal string "all".
implementationPlan.note: Changed sendMessage() return type to { message, routingTargets }. Updated chatSend.ts to return routingTargets instead of routed: message.mentions. Updated decision creation code in StateManager to destructure the new return type.

implementationPlan.description:
P2 — Fix group mention expansion to use team.role lookup.

In `packages/moe-daemon/src/util/mentionRouter.ts`:
- The `parseMentions()` method currently expands `@architects`/`@workers`/`@qa` using `id.toLowerCase().includes(...)` — a fragile heuristic that breaks when worker IDs don't match role names.
- Change `parseMentions()` signature: it already accepts an optional `workers?: Worker[]` param. Add a `teams?: Team[]` param (or receive a `getTeamForWorker` callback).
- For group mentions (`@architects`, `@workers`, `@qa`), resolve by:
  1. For each worker, look up their `teamId` → find that team → check `team.role`.
  2. If `team.role === 'architect'` → worker is an architect, etc.
  3. Keep the ID-based fallback as a secondary match if no teams exist yet (backwards compat).
- In `StateManager.sendMessage()`, pass the teams array (from `this.getTeams()` or similar) to `mentionRouter.route()`.
- Update `MentionRouter.route()` to forward teams to `parseMentions()`.
- Update `packages/moe-daemon/src/util/mentionRouter.test.ts`: add tests for team-role-based group mention expansion. Test: create workers with teamIds, create teams with roles, verify `@architects` resolves to workers on architect teams (not just workers with 'architect' in their ID).
implementationPlan.note: Added Team parameter to parseMentions/route methods. Group mentions (@architects/@workers/@qa) now resolve by team.role lookup with ID substring fallback when no teams exist. StateManager passes teams array to mentionRouter.route(). Added 5 new tests for team-role resolution.

implementationPlan.description:
P3 — Validate sender identity in chat_send.

In `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatSend.ts`:
- Currently any string is accepted as `workerId` — an unregistered agent can impersonate a known worker.
- Before calling `state.sendMessage()`, validate the sender:
  1. If `workerId` is `"human"` or `"system"` → always allowed (these are reserved senders).
  2. Otherwise, call `state.getWorker(workerId)` — if null, return an error: `{ success: false, error: 'invalidInput', message: 'Unknown sender: workerId must be a registered worker, "human", or "system"' }`.
- Use the existing error response pattern from other tools (check `reportBlocked.ts` or `claimNextTask.ts` for the invalidInput error pattern).
- Test: vitest test that attempts chat_send with an unregistered workerId and verifies it returns an invalidInput error. Also test that "human" and "system" senders always pass validation.
implementationPlan.note: Added sender validation before sendMessage call. Reserved senders 'human' and 'system' are always allowed. All other senders must be registered workers via state.getWorker(). Throws invalidInput error for unknown senders.

implementationPlan.description:
P4 — Include chat_wait workers in chat_who channel filter.

In `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatWho.ts`:
- Currently, when a `channel` filter is provided, only workers with `chatCursors[channel]` are included. Workers using only `chat_wait` + `chat_send` (never calling `chat_read`) are invisible.
- Import `activeChatWaiters` from `./chatWait.ts`.
- When filtering by channel: also include workers that have an active waiter for that channel. Check `activeChatWaiters` — each entry has a `channels` filter (or null for all channels). A worker is 'in' a channel if their waiter's channels list includes that channel OR is null/empty (watching all channels).
- Merge the two sets (cursor-based + waiter-based) and deduplicate by workerId.
- Add a `source` field to distinguish presence type: `'cursor'` (has chatCursors entry), `'waiting'` (in chat_wait), or `'both'`.
- Test: vitest test that registers a worker in chat_wait for a specific channel but without chatCursors, then calls chat_who with that channel and verifies the worker appears.
implementationPlan.note: Exported ActiveChatWaiter interface and added channels field. chatWho now does two passes: cursor-based workers + chat_wait workers, with deduplication and source tracking ('cursor', 'waiting', 'both').

implementationPlan.description:
P5 — Add moe.chat_create_channel tool.

Create `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatCreateChannel.ts`:
- Follow the existing tool pattern (see `chatJoin.ts` or `chatPin.ts` for structure).
- Tool name: `moe.chat_create_channel`
- Input schema: `{ name: string (required), type?: 'custom' (default 'custom', only allow 'custom' — epic/task auto-channels are managed internally), linkedEntityId?: string }`
- Handler: call `state.createChannel({ name, type: 'custom', linkedEntityId })` and return the created `ChatChannel` object.
- Validate: channel name must be non-empty. If linkedEntityId is provided, it should be optional metadata (no strict validation needed since custom channels can link to anything).
- Error handling: if `createChannel` throws, return `{ success: false, error: message }`.

Register in `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/index.ts`:
- Import `chatCreateChannelTool` and add it to the tools array alongside the other chat tools.

Test: vitest test that calls the tool handler, verifies a channel is created with the correct name and type='custom', and appears in `state.getChannels()`.
implementationPlan.note: Created chatCreateChannel.ts following existing tool patterns. Only allows type='custom' (epic/task channels are managed internally). Validates name is non-empty, wraps createChannel errors gracefully. Registered in tools/index.ts.

implementationPlan.description:
Verify TypeScript compilation and run all tests.

- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in `packages/moe-daemon/` to verify no type errors.
- Run `npx vitest run` to execute all tests including the new ones.
- Fix any compilation or test failures.
- Ensure existing tests still pass (no regressions).
implementationPlan.note: tsc --noEmit passes cleanly. All 298 tests pass across 16 test files (including 5 new mentionRouter team-role tests). No regressions.

reopenReason:
WebSocketServer.ts SEND_MESSAGE handler sends malformed MESSAGE_SENT payload after sendMessage() return type change.

Line 540: `const msg = await this.state.sendMessage(...)` — `msg` is now `{ message: ChatMessage, routingTargets: string[] }`, not `ChatMessage`.
Line 541: `this.safeSend(ws, JSON.stringify({ type: 'MESSAGE_SENT', payload: { message: msg } }))` — wraps the full result object as `message`, creating nested `message.message` structure.

Clients (VS Code MoeDaemonClient.ts, JetBrains MoeProjectService.kt) expect `MESSAGE_SENT` payload to be `{ message: ChatMessage }`. They receive `{ message: { message: ChatMessage, routingTargets: string[] } }` instead. This breaks chat message sending from both IDE plugins.

FIX: Destructure the return value:
```typescript
const { message } = await this.state.sendMessage({ channel, sender: 'human', content });
this.safeSend(ws, JSON.stringify({ type: 'MESSAGE_SENT', payload: { message } }));
```

All other sendMessage() callers are correct: chatSend.ts (line 47) destructures properly, createDecision (line 968) destructures properly, postSystemMessage/postToGeneralChannel/chatJoin don't use the return value.

failedDodItems: chat_send returns routingTargets (expanded worker IDs) not raw mentions

issues.description: sendMessage() return type changed to { message, routingTargets } but WebSocket SEND_MESSAGE handler at line 540-541 still assigns full result to msg, creating nested message.message payload. Breaks IDE chat message sending.
